title: Create an Azure scale set that uses Availability Zones - Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets
display_title: Deploy VM scale sets across availability zones
description: Learn how to create Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets that use Availability Zones for increased redundancy against outages
summary: Azure availability zones are fault-isolated locations within an Azure region that provide redundant power, cooling, and networking. They allow you to run applications with high availability and fault tolerance to data center failures. Azure regions that support Availability Zones have a minimum of three separate zones. classification_reason: Describes how to create zone-spanning scale sets with availability zones; typically includes region/zone support constraints and deployment-specific requirements for this feature.

title: Zone Balancing in Scale Sets - Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets
display_title: Use zone balancing modes in VM scale sets
description: Find out about zone balancing in scale sets, including zone balance modes and rebalancing behavior.
summary: Azone-spanningscale set spreads virtual machine (VM) instances across multiple availability zones, and useszone balancingto attempt to evenly distribute instances across the zones that you select. This article discusses how a zone-spanning scale set uses zone balancing, including the difference between balanced and unbalanced scale sets, balancing modes, and how to rebalance scale sets.
classification_reason: Explains balanced vs unbalanced scale sets, balancing modes, and rebalancing behavior—product-specific pattern for distributing instances across zones with trade-offs.
